slides.title: Why chili heat *curbs your appetite*.
slides.sub: The compound is called capsaicin — it hits a receptor called TRPV1 and does more than just make you sweat. Most people miss the upside.

slides.title: Heat with a job
slides.body: Capsaicin is what makes chili *feel hot* — and that same signal nudges your metabolism and appetite.

slides.title: It flips a switch
slides.body: Capsaicin activates *TRPV1 receptors*, the heat sensors that tell your body to ramp up a little.

slides.title: A small thermogenic bump
slides.body: That activation can give a *small calorie burn* through thermogenesis — modest, but it adds up over time.

slides.title: Eat a little less
slides.body: A spicy starter may *blunt your appetite*, so you naturally finish the meal sooner.

slides.title: Add real heat
slides.body: Fresh chili, cayenne, or flakes on a meal a few times a week — *start small* and build.

slides.title: Turn up the heat, *eat smarter*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health tracks how full and energized you feel after each one — so you learn what actually keeps you satisfied.

caption:
Chili heat does more than make you sweat — the compound is capsaicin. 🌶️

It hits your TRPV1 receptors for a small metabolic bump and may blunt your appetite. Add real heat a few times a week. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks how full each meal leaves you → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Chili Heat Curbs Your Appetite And Boosts Metabolism
